title: ctenodon soniae
side_effects: Ctenodon soniae can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches due to excessive consumption of its toxic compounds. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can also lead to vomiting, diarrhea, and stomach cramps. Long-term use of the plant may cause liver damage and kidney problems, as well as interact with medications and worsen underlying health conditions. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also increase the risk of seizures and coma in severe cases.
